definitionorgloss.value: baru; belum ada sebelumnya dari yang ada sekarang; belum lama

example.value: Bah Tam kibeli deg pai, tapi pek lei lima gencek naigei ya mong jeoi inerusak.

translation.value: Bah Tam membeli rumah baharu, tetapi tidak sampai sehingga lima bulan mereka meduduki rumah tersebut, telah terdapat banyak kerosakannya.
Bah Tam bought a new house, but in less than five months occupying the house, there was a lot of damage.

definitionorgloss-2.value: masih baharu; belum ada sebelumnya dari yang ada sekarang; belum lama

example-2.value: "Lekat ipeninai lagi ikerite ajeh pek lei nugencek, tapi ya kieksiden kelemnek," Bah Nan kipasal ha kerite iapak nu ikekawat.

translation-2.value: "Keretanya itu masih baharu lagi, belum sampai sebulan tapi sudah mengalami kemalangan semalam," Bah Nan memberitahu kawan-kawannya mengenai kereta bapanya.
"His car is still new, less than a month old but had an accident yesterday," Bah Nan told his friends about his father's car.

example-2.value: Mutu de kigu-pakei ya Bah Lali ajeh kikot ipinai.

translation-2.value: Motosikal yang sering digunakan oleh Bah Lali tersebut adalah motosikal baharu.
The motorcycle that Bah Lali regularly uses is one in a new condition.

definitionorgloss.value: to calculate everything; to be too calculating

example.value: "Wak Sali ajeh kigemar bepajeh ha hik, tapi cuba hik nu ilei pek pernah hibual ma-ma pen," Wak Maria kiurok nu ikekawat.

translation.value: "Wak Sali suka berkira dengan kita, tapi jika kita dengan dirinya, kita tidak pernah berkata apa-apa pun," Wak Maria berkata kepada rakan-rakannya.
"Wak Sali likes to calculate everything that she has spent for us, but if the other way around, we never say a word," Wak Maria told her friends.

example.value: "Baru adehleh kimanik de paleng lebat telas memerip gencek ya pek kimanik begei adeh. Selame anoh, mong kimanik pen had sekejap-sekajap taleh," Bah Kali kibesimbang ru Bah Nua mase ubai pimpoj manik kihoj ku tempat piknoj bas.

translation.value: "Baru kali inilah hujan yang paling lebat setelah berberapa bulan tidak hujan sebegini. Selama ini, hujan hanya turun dengan sekejap-sekejap sahaja," Bah Kali bersembang dengan Bah Nua semasa mereka berdua menunggu hujan reda di tempat perhentian bas.

"This time is the heaviest it has rained after several months of rain unlike it. So far, it has only rained intermittently," Bah Kali chatted with Bah Nua as they both waited for the rain to subside at the bus stop.

for emphasis on the action of a verb

example.value: Wak Mila kipan jar mase biwag ya chok, mase kijog ju sekulah.

translation.value: Wak Mila berlari bersungguh-sungguh semasa dikejar oleh anjing ketika pulang dari sekolah.
Wak Mila ran hard while being chased by a dog on her way home from school.

example.value: Wak Nai kipan jap mase ku danan iamek jop.

translation.value: Wak Nai menangis sekuat-kuatnya semasa kematian neneknya.
Wak Nai cried out loud at the time of her grandmother's death.

example.value: "Agok pan ngut teh ajeh, medei tik beket," Wak Salma kipasal nu ikenon.

translation.value: "Jangan minum teh itu dengan tergesa-gesa kerana ianya masih lagi panas," Wak Salma memberitahu kepada anaknya.
"Don't drink the tea too fast, because it is still hot," Wak Salma told her son.

example.value: Bah Rani pek kisedar ilei kipan besimbang medei ha kiteruja kaha-pasal ikeputusan periksa de cemerlang ajeh nu iamek-apak.

translation.value: Bah Rani tidak menyedari dirinya bersembang dengan sangat kuat kerana dia teruja untuk memberitahu keputusan perperiksaannya yang cemerlang itu kepada ibu-bapanya.
Bah Rani did not realize he was speaking very loud as he was excited to tell his parents the excellent results of his examination.

definitionorgloss.value: pasak; tetap; tinggal di satu tempat sahaja

example.value: "Sebenarnye, jik mai luar tapi ya jipasak ku kampug adeh ya liu, sejak jaman kemoj dot jik lagi," Bah Mali kipasal nu Bah Nan mase ubai besimbang.

translation.value: "Sebenarnya, kami merupakan orang luar tetapi sudah pasak di kampung ini sudah lama, sejak zaman mendiang moyang kami lagi," Bah Mali memberitahu kepada Bah Nan semasa mereka bersembang.
"Actually, we are outsiders but we have been permanently settled in this village for a long time, since the time of our great grandfather," Bah Mali told Bah Nan while they were chatting.

definitionorgloss.value: paskah; salah satu perayaan keagamaan bagi agama Yahudi dan Kristian
passover; easter; one of the religious celebration by the Jewish and Christian

example.value: "Ku senamut raya Paskah doh, jik mong jibeh caknak ku padeg. Amen engkesenang, beile hol cakcak," Wak Ani kipasal nu ikekawat.

translation.value: "Pada sambutan perayaan Paskah nanti, kami mengadakan jamuan di rumah. Jika kalian mempunyai kelapangan, datanglah untuk makan," Wak Ani memberitahu kepada kawan-kawannya.
"At the coming Easter celebration, we will have a feast at home. If you have time, please come and join us," Wak Ani told her friends.

example.value: "Amek eng selalo kipateh icecenok medei rajet naitulog bahbeh kerjak deg," Wak Nita kipasal nu igensir.

translation.value: "Ibu saya selalu memuji cucu-cucunya kerana mereka rajin menolong melakukan kerja-kerja rumah," Wak Nita memberitahu suaminya.
"My mother always praises her grandchildren because they are diligent in helping with housework," Wak Nita told her husband.

example-2.value: "Pek layak ngrasa ngkep penateh ju hek medei eng pai ngkerakjak," Bah Mirul kiurok nu ibos telas bipateh ya ibos ajeh ha ilei ihad rajet kerakjak.

translation-2.value: "Saya merasa tidak layak mendapat pujian dari tuan kerana saya baharu sahaja mulai bekerja," Bah Mirul berkata demikian kepada bosnya setelah dipuji oleh bosnya tersebut bahawa dia sangat rajin bekerja.
"I feel unworthy of praise from you because I have only just started working," Bah Mirul said after being praised by his boss that he is very hard-working.

example.value: "Agok hiran, keneng legos adeh mimang patik medei ha bikawen," Bah Selah kipasal nu ikekawat mase naihol saje naicekcip nu ikebon.

translation.value: "Jangan hairan, pokok rambutan ini memang pendek kerana ianya rambutan kawin," Bah Selah memberitahu rakan-rakannya semasa mereka ke berjalan-jalan ke kebunnya.
"Do not be surprised, this rambutan tree is indeed short because it is grafted," Bah Selah told his friends when they visited his garden.

example.value: Hamapan Bah Jali ajeh itenek ru Bah Mami ajeh imenang, tapi Bah Jali lebeh patik ju Bah Mami.

translation.value: Walaupun Bah Jali adalah abang kepada Bah Mami, tetapi Bah Jali lebih rendah dari Bah Mami.
Although Bah Jali is the older brother of Bah Mami, nevertheless Bah Jali is shorter to Bah Mami.

example.value: Bah Gilan kipatoh, tok pek kidegel engrok iapak de kior akiperdij ibelajar ku universiti canuk sebelom kibekenah.

example.value: Bah Man mong kidek peas ku ideg mahnuh. Mase pai kikep entem, ipeas ajeh had mar tapar teg taleh. Hunen iteninoi ya mar lik entoi.

translation.value: Bah Man ada membela labi-labi (yang disimpan) di bilik mandi. Masa dia baharu mendapatnya, saiznya cuma sebesar tapak tangan sahaja. Sekarang, saiznya sudah sebesar kuali yang besar.

example.value: "Mongka teu peceg? Eng ngha-ngut. Beket betol iarik, arik adeh!" Bah Lan kisemanj nu ikenah telas kijog ju iselai.

translation.value: "Ada air sejuk tak? Saya mahu minum. Cuaca sangat panas hari ini!" Bah Lan meminta kepada isterinya setelah dia pulang dari ladangnya.
"Is there any cold water? I want to drink. The weather is very hot today!" Bah Lan asked his wife after he returned from his farm.

translation.value: Bah Atan tidak mempeduli jika ada sesiapa yang datang ke rumahnya kerana dia memang tinggal bersendirian.

example.value: "Ngoi kak piya ajeh peeg hamapen ya bigureng. Pek lalu ngha-cak," engrok amek Wak Tia.

translation.value: "Bau ikan talipia itu hanyir walaupun ianya sudah digoreng. Tidak boleh saya memakannya," kata ibu kepada Wak Tia.

example.value: "Seminggu sebelom kidat, jik ya jiperasan ilei iperangei ya pelek. Selalo kitemenung ru kibelwal kihalei," Wak Rina kipasal nu ikekawat ha iapak jop.

example.value: "Beket betol ku luar, lei pelit hirasa kena ya pengrag!" Bah Wan kiurok nu ikekawat.

translation.value: "Di luar sekarang sangat panas, sehingga terasa pedih jika terkena pancaran matahari!" Bah Wan berkata kepada kawan-kawannya.
"It's very hot outside now until one feels a stinging pain when exposed to the sun!" Bah Wan said to his friends.

definitionorgloss.value: atap; penutup rumah bahagian atas yang diperbuat daripada zink, daun pokok bertam, daun rumbia, daun nipah, dsbnya.

example.value: "Pelog deg jik de bibeh ju beltop ya peg. Adeh hunen gensir eng ya kiet kibeli pelog zink dea tahan liu gek," Wak Ramlah kipasal nu iamek.

translation.value: "Atap rumah kami yang diperbuat dari bertam sudah bocor. Sekarang suami saya pergi membeli atap zink supaya tahan lebih lama," Wak Ramlah memberitahu ibunya.
"The roof of our house which is made of bertam leaves has leaked. Now my husband is going to buy a zinc roof so that it will last longer," Wak Ramlah told her mother.

definitionorgloss.value: to warm up body by the fire

example.value: Telas ya sudah naibepanah kak, Bah Kudil ru ikekawat naipempom os canuk naiha-berket berog enai sebelom naijog nu madeg.

example.value: "Agok pendak caknak, amen hapanei pek lalu haha-perdij, bahagik nu mai kilek," amek kiurok nu menang jik mase jicakcak ku restoren.

translation.value: "Jangan membazir makanan, jika kamu tahu kamu tidak mampu menghabiskan makanan kamu, berikan separuh kepada orang lain," ibu berkata kepada adik kami semasa kami makan di restoran.

example.value: "Pengres pog amek eng jooi," Bah Uda kisureh nu ikekawat.

example.value: Bah Kamil kinyok rukok ru ikekawat ku piknoj bas ku simpang kampug telas naiceleh ju bas sekulah. Aleh-aleh iapak kilalu ku pajeh. Ku peninen juga Bah Kamil bitampar ya iapak medei bibakek kinyok rukok.

translation.value: Bah Kamil menghisap rokok dengan kawan-kawannya di perhentian bas di simpang kampung setelah turun dari bas sekolah. Tiba-tiba bapanya lalu di tempat itu. Di situ juga dia ditampar oleh bapanya kerana kedapatan dia merokok.

Bah Kamil was smoking cigarettes with his friends at the bus stop, at the village junction after getting off the school bus. Suddenly his father passed by that place. At the very same place his father slapped him because he found him smoking.

example.value: "Eng ngha-gei ku peninoh taleh, ngsegat jekjeg nu diteh. Pekma nggei nghalei, hamapen nyak ju mai kilek," ajok kiurok nu amek jik mase ilei bipujok dea kigei ru jik.

translation.value: "Saya mahu tinggal di sini sahaja, saya malas untuk berpindah ke sana. Tidak mengapa jika saya tinggal bersendirian, walaupun jauh dari orang lain," nenek berkata kepada ibu kami semasa dia dipujuk untuk tinggal bersama-sama dengan kami.

"I want to stay just here, I'm too lazy to move over there. It does not matter if I live alone, even far from other people," our grandmother said to our mother as she was being persuaded to live with us.

definitionorgloss.value: Pentakosta; salah satu dari perayaan utama agama Kristian

example.value: Pentakosta adeh raya mai Kristian de naisamut ha peringatan teniba Kelog Santen nu pengikot-pengikot Yesus.

translation.value: Pentakosta adalah perayaan orang Kristian yang mereka sambut bagi mengingati kedatangan Roh Kudus ke atas pengikut-pengikut Yesus.

en: to cook food by using a bamboo stem as a container to keep the food while heating up
"Chicken that is put in a bamboo stem and then roasted is delicious," Cikgu Muhammad said to Cikgu Azman.

example.value: "Pep itemagik, beketka tok?" Bah Derus kipasal nu ikenah dea kirasa kenon ubai ajeh kinyaknyikka tok.

translation.value: "Sentuh dahinya, panaskah tidak?" Bah Derus memberitahu kepada isterinya bagi mengetahui anak mereka demam atau tidak.
"Touch his forehead, is it hot?" Bah Derus told his wife to find out if their child had a fever.

example.value: Wak Jalina kibaca pepa kelemnek, medei kelemnek pek sempat kibaca.

example.value: Pepegelap Bah Tam ya kikus kaha-et nu mutong.

translation.value: Awal-awal pagi lagi Bah Tam sudah bangun dari tidur untuk  pergi menoreh.
Bah Tam already woke up at dawn to go rubber tapping.

example.value: Pepegelap lagi Bah Jan ya kihol ju ikendeg kiet nu mutong.

translation.value: Awal pagi-pagi lagi Bah Jan sudah keluar dari rumah untuk menoreh getah.

definitionorgloss-2.value: menutup (tidur atau sekadar untuk melelapkan mata)

example-2.value: Bah Midan kiperbet imat, pure-pure kibukbet mase bicereg ya iamek medei bior et cah kebon ceber.

translation-2.value: Bah Midan memejamkan matanya, berpura-pura sedang tidur semasa dipanggil oleh ibunya yang menyuruh dia pergi menebas semak dan lalang di kebun getah.
Bah Midan closed his eyes, pretending to be asleep when he was being called by his mother who told him to go and cut down the bushes and weeds at the rubber plantation.

definitionorgloss-2.value: berpergian, berjalan ke tempat yang jauh
membunuh; mematikan; mengambil nyawa seseorang atau sesuatu

example-2.value: Bah Jali kiperdat pelep de kikap ha ijog ru yoj.

translation-2.value: Bah Jali membunuh pacat yang menggigit kakinya dengan pisau.
Bah Jali killed the leach that bit his leg with a knife.

example-2.value: Wak Jangkor kiperder os medei kaha-rag kak.

translation-2.value: Wah Jangkor menyalakan api kerana mahu membakar ikan.
Wah Jangkor lit a fire because she wanted to grill some fish.

definitionorgloss-2.value: to dress someone up; to put on (of clothes)

example-2.value: "Perlog baju cereg iteg nu Bah Zack, doh kisengij," Wak Lila kipasal nu igensir.

translation-2.value: "Pakaikan baju yang berlengan panjang kepada Bah Zack, jika tidak nanti dia akan sejuk," Wak Lila berkata kepada suaminya.
"Put a long-sleeved shirt on Bah Zack, otherwise he will be cold," Wak Lila said to her husband.

definitionorgloss-2.value: to display; to show; to reveal

example-2.value: Bah Rilan kiperneng nu ikekawat dil keknap cok ku lempak.

translation-2.value: Bah Rilan menunjukkan kepada kawan-kawannya bekas gigitan anjing yang terdapat pada peha.
Bah Rilan showed his friends the marks of the dog bite that were on his thigh.

definitionorgloss.value: tempias; air hujan yang termasuk sedikit ke dalam sesuatu (rumah, bangunan, kenderaan, dll)

example.value: "Tutop tingkap ajeh doh perai nu kateh deg," Wak Mila kipasal nu imenang mase iarik ya mula kimanik.

translation.value: "Tutup tingkap tersebut, jika tidak akan tempias ke dalam rumah," Wak Mila memberitahu adiknya semasa hujan mulai turun.
"Close the window, otherwise the rain will splash into the house," Wak Mila told her sister as it started to rain.

example.value: "Jok hijog aderas, peralit ngrasa bikap ya kemet!" engrok Wak Tina nu itenek mase baileh lada ku kebon.

translation.value: "Jom segera pulang, saya rasa gatal (yang memedihkan) digigit oleh nyamuk!" Wak Tina berkata kepada kakaknya semasa mereka berdua memetik lada di kebun.
"Let's go home immediately, I feel itchy (which is painful) because I have been bitten by a mosquito!" Wak Tina said to her sister as they both picked peppers in the garden.

definitionorgloss.value: satu persatu; satu demi satu mengikut giliran; tiap satu

example.value: "Ajok eng ya kiengrak, iumur ya 88 tahut. Ilei kenon de iloj ru kihalei taleh de lekat sui ju dikidj iberadek. Dij kirasa peranek-peranek itetenek naidat," Bah Miran kipasal nu ikenah mase ubai becerite ha keluarge liubai memaseg.

translation.value: "Nenek saya sudaha tua, umurnya sudah 88 tahun. Dia anak bongsu dan hanya dia seorang sahaja yang masih hidup di antara adik-beradiknya. Dia merasai setiap kehilangan satu persatu abang-abang dan kakak-kakaknya," Bah Miran memberitahu isterinya semasa mereka bercerita mengenai keluarga mereka masing-masing.

"My grandmother is old, she is 88 years old. She is the youngest child and she is the only one that is still alive among the siblings. One by one, she experienced the loss of each of her siblings," Bah Miran told his wife as they talked about their respective families.

example.value: Bah Mali ihad kibelal ru perangei ikenon de pek raktik. Ku kedei caknak ikenon ajeh kijerjar ditah-madeh, langsong pek buleh kigei tempat.

translation.value: Bah Mali amat marah dengan perangai anaknya yang tidak faham bahasa. Anaknya tersebut berlari ke sana-sini di restoran dan tidak boleh duduk setempat.
Bah Mali is very angry with his son's behaviour who did not listen to him. He ran all over the place at the restaurant and could not sit in one place.

example.value: Bah Sam pek kitutop tingkap mase manik, jadi ibilek keaj ya perasoi teu manik.

translation.value: Bah Sam tidak menutup tingkap biliknya semasa hujan turun, jadi biliknya basah kerana tempias air hujan.
Bah Sam did not close the window of his room when it rained, so his room was wet due to the rainwater splashing in.

example.value: "Bor hijog canuk jampong lekat perasoi, doh ya manik lebat susah hiha-jog madeg," Bah Dali kipasal nu ikekawat mase enai ku padag bula.

translation.value: "Baik kita harus pulang sekarang ke rumah sementara hujan masih renyai, jika hujan sudah lebat, susah kita hendak pulang ke rumah," Bah Dali memberitahu rakan-rakannya semasa mereka di padang bolasepak.
"We had better go home now while the rain is still drizzling, if it rains heavily, it will be difficult," Bah Dali told his friends while they were at the football field.

example.value: "Waktu senanget jik, jik selalo jiperat krekbag ya abat warne oren," ajok jik kibecerite nu jik ha mase isenanget.

translation.value: "Semasa kami masih kanak-kanak, kami selalu mengumpan rama-rama dengan menggunakan kain berwarna jingga," nenek bercerita kepada kami mengenai zaman kanak-kanaknya.
"When we were kids, we always lured the butterflies with an orange cloth," my grandmother told us about the time when she was a child.

example.value: Cikgu Ramli kiperatik ianak muret naimain bula, kaha-panei ilei belok de buleh kipileh sebegei pemain sekulah.

translation.value: Cikgu Ramli memerhatikan anak-anak muridnya bermain bola sepak bagi mengetahui yang mana satu yang boleh dipilih sebagai pemain sekolah.
The teacher Mr. Ramli carefully watched his students playing football to find out which one could represent the school.

displayXhtml: perekrek

example.value: "Agok sangka kibet ajeh, had kiperekbet taleh medei kisegat bohha-kos ju ibuknet. Et beryol ya hek dea kikos!" amek Bah Selan kiurok nu Bah Ian mase Bah Ian kiet nu deg Bah Selan, kaha-ajak hol nu pekan.

translation.value: "Jangan menyangka bahawa dia tidur, dia berpura-pura tidur sahaja kerana malas untuk bangun dari tidur. Pergi kejutkan dia supaya dia bangun!" ibu Bah Selan berkata Bah Ian semasa Bah Ian pergi ke rumah Bah Selan kerana mahu mengajaknya keluar ke pekan.

"Don't imagine that he is sleeping, he is just pretending to sleep because he doesn't want to wake up. Go and wake him up so he will get up!" Bah Selan's mother said Bah Ian when Bah Ian went to Bah Selan's house because he wanted to take him out to town.

definitionorgloss.value: menunjuk-nunjuk; memperlihat sesuatu kepada orang lain bagi tujuan untuk berlagak
to show off; to display someone or something with the object of boasting

example.value: Mai kampug naiperasan Bah Wan kibeh diteh-madeh ku nong kampug medei kaha-perekneng ha imutu pai. Tapi, naibeh pek naibakek taleh kerana ya naipanei ilei mimang begei ajeh.

translation.value: Penduduk kampung telah perasan bahawa Bah Wan telah berberapa kali ke hulu-hilir jalan kampung tersebut dengan tujuan untuk menunjuk-nunjuk motosikal baharunya. Tetapi, mereka berpura-pura tidak nampak kerana sudah mengetahui bahawa beliau memang berperangai sebegitu.

The villagers noticed that Bah Wan had gone back and forth on the village's road with the aim of showing off his new motorcycle. But, they pretended not to see him because they already knew that he is that kind of person.

example.value: "Hampen hunen ya moden, ajok eng lekat pek kipecaya perembor ya dokte ku kedei. Ilei lekat kipecaya nu penglai serag begei ha herba," Wak Mila kipasal nu ikekawat.

translation.value: "Walaupun sekarang sudah maju, nenek saya masik tidak mempercayai pengubatan dari doktor di pekan. Dia masih mempercayai ubatan tradisi dari hutan seperti herba-herba," Wak Mila memberitahu rakan-rakannya.

"Even though now we live in the modern age, my grandmother still does not trust the treatment from the doctors in the town. She still believes in traditional medicines from the forest like herbs," Wak Mila told her friends.

definitionorgloss.value: orang yang membuat sesuatu menjadi hidup atau berfungsi
one who cause something to come to life or to function

example.value: "Bok iperengsui mesin jehuk ajeh nek?" Bah Lalil kitanya nu ikekawat kerjak.

translation.value: "Siapakah yang menghidupkan mesin kayu itu?" Bah Lalil bertanya kepada kawan-kawan kerjanya.
"Who is the person who started the woodworking machine on?" Bah Lalil asked his co-workers.

example.value: Wak Jah ru Bah Dan mong ubai peres kenon angkat medei liubai pek bekenun hamapen ya liu ubai bekenah-gengsir.

example.value: Bah Bob kien ikenah perilai nu halak ku Cameron Highland ha nyaknik ikenah de lumpoh pek bor-bor hamapen ya kien nu dokte.

example.value: "Kampug jik lalek, had mong 6 peringak taleh ku kampug jik," Wak Eri kipasal bile bitanya ya ikekawat.

translation.value: "Kampung kami kecil, hanya ada 6 keluarga sahaja di kampung kami," Wak Eri memberitahu apabila ditanya oleh rakan-rakannya.
"Our village is small, there are only 6 families in our village," Wak Eri said when asked by her friends.

example.value: "Mahkamah ya kiperhol perintah or pekusong deg adeh ku arik adeh juga medei pek dapat hipelangsei ihutag," Bah Tali kipasal nu ikenon-kenah.

translation.value: "Mahkamah sudah mengeluarkan perintah menyuruh rumah ini dikosongkan pada hari ini juga kerana kita tidak dapat melangsaikan hutang rumah ini," Bah Tali memberitahu kepada anak-anak dan isterinya.
"The court has issued an order that this house be vacated today because we cannot settle the debt (of this house)," Bah Tali told his wife and children.

to familiarize; to get used to

example.value: "Agok perlej kenon hek ru bende mewah dea naijadi sengoi de sederhana doh," amek kiurok nu tenek eng.

translation.value: "Jangan membiasakan anak-anak kamu dengan benda-benda yang mewah, supaya mereka nanti menjadi orang yang sederhana," ibu berkata kepada abang saya.
"Don't let your children get used to luxury items. If you don't, they will later be unpretentious," the mother said to my brother.

example.value: Bah Kamil kipes ibilek medei kaha-lai idaknak.

translation.value: Bah Kamil menyapu biliknya kerana mahu membentangkan tempat tidurnya.
Bah Kamil swept his bedroom because he wanted to spread out a place to sleep.

example-2.value: "Tulog og penes ajeh, eng ngha-pes deg wal," Wak Rina kiseman nu ikenon.

translation-2.value: "Tolong ambil penyapu tersebut, saya mahu menyapu dapur," Wak Rina meminta pada anaknya.
"Please take the broom, I want to sweep the kitchen," Wak Rina asked her son.

example-2.value: Wak Embong kipispes ideg medei ya nik arik pek kipes ideg ajeh medei kisibok kerakjak. Ku arik ajeh kek kicuti ikerjak, jadi kikot mase kikemas ideg.

translation-2.value: Wak Embong menyapu rumahnya kerana sudah tiga hari dia tidak menyapu rumahnya tersebut kerana sibuk bekerja. Pada hari tersebut dia cuti dari pekerjaannya, jadi dia mengambil masa tersebut untuk mengemas rumahnya.
Wak Embong swept her house because he had not swept her house for three days, as she was busy with work. On that day she was on leave from work, so she took the time to tidy up her house.

translation.value: "Kamu hendaklah memberikan teladan yang baik, kerana pada masa akan datang kamulah yang menjadi contoh kepada golongan muda yang kemudian," kata Cikgu Rama yang memberikan motivasi kepada pelajar-pelajar sekolah menengah.
"You should set a good example, because later in the future you will be a role model to the young generation," said Cikgu Rama who was giving a motivational talk to the high school students.

definitionorgloss.value: to pay attention; to observe; to watch

example.value: "Ju cinek keral ajeh kipijoi ha hik. Tah yama?" Wak Lila kipasal nu ikekawat mase tengah naipoj bas.

translation.value: "Dari tadi dia memerhatikan kita. Entah kenapa?" Wak Lila memberitahu kepada rakan-rakannya semasa mereka sedang menunggu bas.
"He has been watching us for a while. Why, I don't know," Wak Lila told her friends while they were waiting for the bus.

example.value: Bah Karat pek jemu-jemu kiulag neng pilem P. Ramlee. Amen mong mase, kek mesti kineng pilem-pilem de bilakon ya P. Ramlee.

definitionorgloss.value: nama panggilan bagi anak lelaki di tengah, iaitu bagi anak yang bukan bongsu dan bukan sulung
nickname of the middle son, which is not the youngest nor the oldest son

example.value: "Eng mong nik urag kenon. Wak Long, Bah Piog ru Bah Luj," kipasal ya Wak Nani bile bitanya ya ikawat kerjak ku tempat kerjak pai.

translation.value: "Saya mempunyai tiga orang anak, iaitu Wak Long, Bah Piog dan Bah Luj," Wak Nani memberitahu apabila ditanya oleh kawan sekerja di tempat kerjanya yang baharu.
"I have three children, namely Wak Long, Bah Piog and Bah Luj," Wak Nani said when asked by a colleague at her new workplace.

example.value: "Piog, bulehka hatulog angkat arap eng adeh, pek nglalu medei ya ngengrak," ajok Bah Sali kiurok nu litau de kibakek ku simpang kampug.

translation.value: "Nak, bolehkah kamu tolong angkat barang saya ini, saya tidak larat kerana sudah tua," nenek kepada Bah Sali berkata kepada pemuda yang dia nampak di simpang kampung.
"Son, can you please lift my belongings, I am not strong enough because I am old," Bah Sali's grandmother said to the young man she saw at the village's entrance.

example.value: Bah Duan kitejaga ju ibuknet medei kikekjet ha suara Bah Pani de tiba-tiba kipipui mase naibukbet.

translation.value: Bah Duan terjaga dari tidurnya kerana terkejut dengan suara Bah Pani yang tiba-tiba mengigau semasa mereka sedang tidur.
Bah Duan woke up from his sleep because he was shocked by Bah Pani who suddenly became delirious (and started raving) while they were sleeping.

example.value: "Hek adeh kenon bok?" Bah Dul kitanya. Bah Rani kiberlag, "Eng adeh pitak Bah Lobang ju Kampar."

translation.value: "Kamu anak siapa?" Bah Bul bertanya. Bah Rani menjawab, " Saya dari puak Bah Lobang, yang dari Kampar."

definitionorgloss.value: party; a specific group of people

example.value: "Adeh ya seminggu pitak aska naibeh latihan ku serag kampug jik, tapi pèq jipanei lei bile kaha-dij," Bah Man kipasal nu ikekawat.

translation.value: "Sekarang sudah seminggu pihak askar membuat latihan di hutan di kampung kami, tapi kami tidak mengetahui sampai bila akan tamat," Bah Man memberitahu rakan-rakannya.
"It's been a week now that the soldiers have been training in the forest at our village, but we do not know when they will finish," Bah Man told his friends.

example.value: "Mong kerite kieksiden kelemnek mase ngha-jog madeg ju tempat kerjak. Rimdij sengoi ju kerite ajeh planteg nu luar! Besengoh hineng, mong behip majeh-manen!" Wak Rika kipasal nu ikekawat ku tempat kerjak.

translation.value: "Terdapat kereta mengalami kemalangan semasa saya mahu pulang dari tempat kerja semalam. Kesemua orang dari kereta tersebut tercampak ke luar kereta! Sangat menakutkan kerana tempat tersebut berlumuran dengan darah!" Wak Rika memberitahu kawan-kawan di tempat kerjanya.

"There was a car that had an accident when I was about to go home from work yesterday. Everyone from the car was thrown out of the car! It was scary because the place was covered in blood!" Wak Rika told her friends at the workplace.

definitionorgloss.value: pocong; sejenis hantu, iaitu mayat yang baharu dikebumikan dan masih berbungkus dengan kain kapan
shroud ghost; a kind of ghost relating to a corpse that has just been buried and is still wrapped and therefore trapped in its shroud

example.value: "Bok imai taleh de nailalu ku nong ajeh mesti naibekek pocong, medei ku pajeh mong penep Gop," Bah Kurat kiserngoh ha icecenok de selalo naihol ru naicekcip ku arik kelem.

translation.value: "Sesiapa sahaja yang melalui jalan tersebut pasti akan ternampak pocong, kerana di situ terdapat perkuburan Islam," Bah Kurat menakut-nakutkan cucu-cucunya kerana mereka sering keluar dan berjalan-jalan di waktu malam.
"Anyone who passes by the road will definitely see a pocong, because there is an Islamic cemetery there," Bah Kurat frightened his grandchildren because they often going out at night.

definitionorgloss.value: membuka sesuatu bekas yang berpenutup seperti tupperware
to open a container that has a lid like tupperware

example.value: Wak Imah pek lalu kaha-pog tuntop mangkok de pai kiperhol ju peti ais medei ya melekat ya caknak de mong ku ikateh. Jadi kirendam canuk mangkok ya teu beket.

translation.value: Wak Imah tidak berdaya untuk membuka penutup mangkuk yang baharu beliau keluarkan dari peti sejuk kerana ianya sudah melekat oleh makanan yang ada di dalamnya. Jadi, dia merendam mangkuk tersebut terlebih dahulu dengan air panas.

Wak Imah was not able to open the lid of the food container that she had just taken out of the fridge because it was stuck to the food that was inside it. So, she soaked the container with a hot water.

example-2.value: Wak Gina kicog ipog entag ya sunteg.

definitionorgloss.value: to blow (of wind, not human agency)

example.value: "Sendap bipoi ya pinoi," kiurok ya Bah Miran mase kigigei ku kerop jehuk ru ikekawat.

translation.value: "Sedapnya ditiup oleh angin," Bah Miran berkata semasa sedang duduk di bawah pokok dengan rakan-rakannya.
"The wind feels good," Bah Miran said while sitting under a tree with his friends.

example-2.value: Wak Senah ru ikekenon naigei ku luar deg, ku kerop jehuk ku dui ajeh medei beket ku kateh deg, pek bepinoi.

translation-2.value: Wak Senah dan anak-anaknya duduk di luar rumah, di bawah pokok pada waktu petang kerana merasa panas di dalam rumah akibat tiada angin.
Wak Senah and her children sat outside the house, under a tree during the evening because they felt hot inside the house without any air current.

translation.value: Bah Ruma mahu ke kedai kerana mahu menukar pam yang baharu dibelinya tersebut, kerana pam tersebut sudah rosak.
Bah Ruma wanted to go to the store because he wanted to switch the pump he had just bought, because it was damaged.

example.value: "Maap, lingah jilei. Bepuseng jicip, tapi pek jibakek nong nu madeh," apak jik kiurok nu ibos mase mong jamuan ku deg ibos ajeh.

translation.value: "Maaf, kami lambat sampai. Berpusing-pusing kami mencari, tetapi tidak dapat menjumpai jalan untuk ke sini," bapa berkata kepada majikannya semasa terdapat jamuan di rumah majikannya tersebut.
"Sorry, we arrived late. We kept circling and searching, but could not find the way here," our father said to his employer during a banquet at his employer's house.

definitionorgloss.value: to shoot with a blowpipe; to blow a blowpipe

example.value: Bah Keli pai kibelajar pekput, jadi kiput cep ku keneng legos tapi pek lei.

translation.value: Bah Keli baharu belajar menyumpit, jadi dia menyumpit burung yang terdapat di pokok rambutan, tetapi ianya tidak sampai ke sasaran.
Bah Keli just learned to hunt using a blow pipe, so he blew a blowpipe at a bird in the rambutan tree, but it did not reach the target.

example-2.value: Bah Jali ru ikekawat naiet naipekput nu darat.

translation-2.value: Bah Jali dan rakan-rakannya pergi menyumpit ke hutan.
Bah Jali and his friends went hunting to the forest using a blowpipe.
